checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. Information is presented in the same logical sequence, with headings, paragraphs, and tables appearing in the same relative positions. The addition of a navigation sidebar is an expected responsive design element and does not detract from the visual fidelity of the document's core content.

All tables from the source PDF, including the workshop schedule (Table 1), the study group list (Table 2), population data (Table 3, Appendix B), call volumes (Table 4), complex financial spreadsheets (FY 2021-2023), and the goal/objective tables (Goals 1-10), are rendered as properly structured and readable HTML tables. Borders and column alignments are preserved, making the data easy to interpret.

Meaningful images found in the source PDF are correctly present in the HTML output. This includes the North Carolina flag on the cover page, the Ritter Strategic Services logo, and the conceptual system diagram (Figure 1). There are no broken image placeholders or missing diagrams that carry content meaning.

A thorough review of the HTML screenshot shows no instances of body content (paragraphs, tables, or sections) being erroneously duplicated. While headings appear in both the navigation sidebar and the main content area, this is a standard accessibility feature and not a defect. The goals are listed in the Executive Summary and then detailed later in tables, which faithfully replicates the structure of the source PDF.
